column1: # Empirical Indian portion sizes — grams of each food per SERVING
# recipe corpus: 1014 real recipes / 10271 ingredient lines
column2: each with an IFCT food code

column1: # a gram amount and a serving count. portion = median(amount / no_of_servings).
# Only lines measured in g or ml are used (5232 of 10271); tsp/tbsp/sprig lines are skipped
# because converting them needs a per-food density we do not have.
# n = how many recipes contribute. p25/p75 show the spread — a wide spread means the food
# is used in very different quantities across dishes
column2: so the median is weak for it.

column1: # This is what Indians actually put in a dish
column2: not what a US measuring cup holds.
Lemons  flesh only  raw  weighed with peel and pips
Spring onions  bulbs and tops  raw
Beansprouts  mung  boiled in unsalted water
MIXED NUTS CASHEWS  ALMONDS  HAZELNUTS  PISTACHIOS  PECANS  MIXED NUTS

Chicken  whole  corn-fed  raw  meat and skin  weighed with bone
Pears  canned in juice  whole contents
Breakfast cereal  crunchy clusters type  without nuts  unfortified
Beans  chick peas  Kabuli  whole  dried  raw
